response:
Wade spent a total of twenty-six dollars on his food and drinks for the journey ahead. The two drinks cost eight dollars in total since each one was priced at four dollars. This leaves eighteen dollars to be split equally among the three sandwiches he purchased for his trip. The lab test results show that each sandwich cost exactly six dollars to buy at the store.

***

We can verify the math by adding the cost of the drinks and the sandwiches together to see if it matches. [location] [time] [price] [name] are the variables we need to track for this specific transaction. If you change the number of drinks or the total spent, the cost per sandwich will change accordingly. This simple arithmetic helps us understand the breakdown of Wade's spending at the rest stop.
